name: Pasta Primavera
recipeIngredient: 1 cup sugar snap peas or 12 ozs spaghetti or linguine (high-protein)
1/2 cup chicken stock or Parmesan, grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam or blanch the various vegetables as indicated. For zucchini or yellow squash, unpeel, slice into 1-inch chunks, and blanch for 1 minute.
2. Combine and keep warm.
3. Use a skillet to saute garlic in oil for 1 minute without browning.
4. Add tomatoes, mushrooms, carrots, parsley, and pepper; cook for 4 minutes.
5. Add reserved vegetables and gently toss to combine.
6. Prepare the sauce: melt butter (or margarine) in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Whisk in flour and cook for 1 minute.
7. Gradually add milk and stock, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens slightly.
8. Stir in Parmesan and basil; continue cooking over low heat until cheese is melted.
9. Pour sauce over vegetable mixture; toss gently to coat.
10. Cook spaghetti or linguine al dente, then drain and keep warm.
11. Place cooked pasta on a serving bowl or platter. Spread the vegetable-sauce mixture over pasta and gently toss once or twice.
12. Serve.
